-module(solver).

%% Helper function to check if a list is sorted in non-decreasing order.
is_sorted(List) ->
    lists:sort(List) == List.

%% Helper function to check if a sub-array can be sorted by circular shifts.
%% A sub-array can be sorted by circular shifts if and only if it is a cyclic permutation
%% of the sorted version of itself.
can_be_sorted_by_shifts(SubArray) ->
    SortedSubArray = lists:sort(SubArray),
    length(SubArray) =:= length(SortedSubArray),
    is_cyclic_permutation(SubArray, SortedSubArray).

%% Helper function to check if ListA is a cyclic permutation of ListB.
is_cyclic_permutation(ListA, ListB) ->
    L1 = length(ListA),
    L2 = length(ListB),
    L1 =:= L2,
    ListA /= [],
    % Concatenate ListB with itself: ListB ++ ListB
    ListB_Double = ListB ++ ListB,
    % Check if ListA is a sublist of ListB_Double
    lists:is_sublist(ListA, ListB_Double).

%% Helper function to find all divisors of N.
divisors(N) ->
    findall(D, (D <- [1..floor(sqrt(N))], rem(N, D) =:= 0,
                 (D =:= N/D -> D; true);
                 (D =:= N/D -> N/D; true)),
    lists:sort(lists:delete(N, [D || D <- [1..floor(sqrt(N))], rem(N, D) =:= 0, D =:= N/D])) ++
    lists:sort([N/D || D <- [1..floor(sqrt(N))], rem(N, D) =:= 0, N/D =/= D]).

%% Main solver function.
solve(nums) ->
    N = length(nums),
    SortedNums = lists:sort(nums),
    
    Divisors = divisors(N),
    
    PossibleK = [K || K <- Divisors, is_valid_k(N, nums, SortedNums, K)],
    
    lists:sum(PossibleK).

%% Checks if a divisor K is a "sortable integer".
is_valid_k(N, nums, SortedNums, K) ->
    % 1. Check if the array can be partitioned into K-sized blocks. (This is guaranteed since K is a divisor of N)
    % 2. Check if each block can be independently sorted into its target sorted configuration.

    Blocks = split_into_k_sized_chunks(nums, K),
    
    IsPossible = true,

    SortedBlocks = [can_be_sorted_by_shifts(Block) || Block <- Blocks],
    
    % If all blocks can be cyclically shifted to their sorted form, we need to ensure that

    TargetSortedBlocks = [lists:sort(Block) || Block <- Blocks],
    TargetConcatenated = lists:flatten(TargetSortedBlocks),
    
    % The crucial insight is that if *every* block can be sorted internally, the resulting
    % array will be sorted if and only if the elements within each block, when sorted,
    % match the corresponding segments of the overall sorted array.
    
    % Let S_i be the i-th sorted block. We need S_1 ++ S_2 ++ ... ++ S_m = SortedNums.
    
    IsConcatenationCorrect = 
        lists:equal(TargetConcatenated, SortedNums),

    % Also, the problem statement implies that if K=1, it only works if the array is already sorted.
    if K =:= 1 -> 
        is_sorted(nums)
    else 
        IsConcatenationCorrect
    end.

split_into_k_sized_chunks(List, K) ->

    end.

lists:take(_N, []) -> [];
lists:take(N, [H|T]) -> [H|lists:take(N-1, T)].

lists:drop(_N, []) -> [];

%% Utility to check if two lists are equal.
lists:equal(L1, L2) -> 
    length(L1) =:= length(L2),
    lists:equal_list(L1, L2).

%% Utility to check if a list is already sorted.

-module(solution).

%% solve(list[int]) -> int
solve(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    SortedNums = lists:sort(Nums),
    
    % Find all divisors of N
    Divisors = find_divisors(N),
    
    % Check each divisor k
    TotalSum = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, K) ->
        if is_sortable(Nums, SortedNums, K) ->
            Acc + K;
        true ->
            Acc
        end
    end, 0, Divisors),
    
    TotalSum.

%% Helper to find all divisors of N
find_divisors(N) ->
    findall(D, (D > 0, D =< N, mod(N, D) =:= 0), Divisors).

%% Helper to check if k is a sortable integer
% is_sortable(Nums, SortedNums, K)
is_sortable(Nums, SortedNums, K) ->
    if K == 1 ->
        % If K=1, each sub-array has 1 element. No rearrangement possible unless already sorted.
        lists:seq_equal(Nums, SortedNums)
    else
        % Divide Nums into blocks of size K
        Blocks = split_into_blocks(Nums, K),
        
        % Check if the set of elements in each block matches the set of elements 
        % in the corresponding block of the sorted array, AND 
        % if the elements within each block can be sorted independently to match
        % the required sorted order.
        
        % Since we can freely permute elements within a block of size K,
        % a block can be rearranged to match *any* permutation of its original elements.
        % For the whole array to become SortedNums, the *set* of elements in each block 
        % of Nums must be exactly the *set* of elements in the corresponding block 
        % of SortedNums.
        
        BlocksSorted = split_into_blocks(SortedNums, K),
        
        lists:same_size(Blocks, BlocksSorted), % Should always be true if K divides N
        
        lists:all(fun(B1, B2) ->
            % Check if the multisets are equal. Sorting and comparing is easiest.
            lists:sort(B1) == lists:sort(B2)
        end, Blocks, BlocksSorted)
    end.

%% Helper to split a list into chunks of size K
split_into_blocks([], _K) -> [];
split_into_blocks(List, K) ->
    Chunk = lists:take(K, List),
    Rest = lists:drop(K, List),
    [Chunk | split_into_blocks(Rest, K)].

%% Helper to check if two lists are equal element-wise
lists:seq_equal(List1, List2) ->
    lists:lists:equal(List1, List2).

%% Helper to check if two lists have the same length
lists:same_size(List1, List2) ->
    length(List1) == length(List2).

%% Helper to take first K elements
lists:take(K, List) ->
    lists:take(K, List).

%% Helper to drop first K elements
lists:drop(K, List) ->
    lists:drop(K, List).

is_k_sortable(K, N, Nums, SortedNums) ->

        % It can only be sorted if it's already sorted.
        lists:seq_equal(Nums, SortedNums)
    else
